title: Fiddanl

'''Fiddanl''', originally known as '''96NK3F2''', was a barren [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]].

==Description==
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]], due to its proximity to the [[Star/Legends|sun]], had a very dense, spherical core consisting of [[Gold/Legends|gold]]-[[Platinum/Legends|platinum]] alloys and other pure [[Ore/Legends|ores]]. The upper mantle was a shallow layer of liquid [[Mercury/Legends|mercury]], which reflected much of the [[Yavin/Legends|sun's]] light.<ref name="GG2" /> The planet's continental plates were constantly shifting atop an underground sea of [[Mercury/Legends|mercury]], sometimes up to 630 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] a day. There were 18 continent-sized land-masses on the surface of the planet, which constantly tore away pieces of each other; though they constantly re-grew through rapid crystal growth on the underside. Numerous smaller islands drifted between the continents, but rarely survived very long.<ref name="GG2" />

Compared to the sea, the continents were relatively light, consisting of [[Cinnabar/Legends|cinnabar]], [[sulfite]], and [[Manganese/Legends|manganese]] compounds and plain rock, which gave them a color pattern of [[Color/Legends|red]], orange, purple and gray. Due to the constant shifting of the continents and a high gravity, the terrain was flat with no hills.

The atmosphere was relatively thin but was stable with airborne nitrogen and sulfur compounds precipitating excessive mercury that boiled up in small explosions. The thin atmosphere caused storms that appeared in average strength and numbers.<ref name="GG2" />

==History==
When the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] was surveyed during the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Galactic Expansion era]], Fiddanl was charted as 96NK3F2. Two passes were made, but none found anything interesting except for the rapid crystal growth beneath the planet's continents.<ref name="GG2" />

It was not until the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]], that a [[Nuiwit]] file clerk in the [[Imperial Xenodetic Survey department]] named the planet after a popular pet name.<ref name="GG2" />

Since the planet was toxic to almost all lifeforms, far away from major trade routes and had little important resources, there had been no interests in starting a mining operation.<ref name="GG2" />

==Appearances==
*[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] {{Imo}}
